personalized.title: Personalized Mode

guest.title: Continue as Guest
guest.description: Try the assistant instantly — no account needed.
guest.bullet2: All data stays on this device
guest.bullet3: Session clears when you close the browser
guest.bullet4: Perfect for a quick first look

trust.title: Your data, your control
trust.body: Guest mode stores everything locally on your device. Nothing is sent to a server. When you're ready for a full experience, personalized mode will sync securely across devices.
trust.point1: No account required for guest mode

landing.privacyNote: We respect your privacy. Guest data never leaves your device.

anmeldungWhat.title: What is Anmeldung?
anmeldungWhat.description: Why city registration matters and when you must do it.

anmeldungDocs.title: Documents for Anmeldung
anmeldungDocs.description: Passport, rental contract, and landlord confirmation.

anmeldungAppointment.title: Book Bürgeramt appointment
anmeldungAppointment.description: Find your local Bürgeramt and schedule a slot.

anmeldungDeadline.title: 14-day deadline
anmeldungDeadline.description: Register within two weeks of moving in.

workPermit.title: Work permit basics
workPermit.description: When you need employer sponsorship vs. open access.

workProcess.title: Work permit process
workProcess.description: Employer, Ausländerbehörde, and start date coordination.

explore.title: Explore topics
explore.description: Browse common bureaucratic tasks and next steps.

generalDocs.title: Document checklist
generalDocs.description: Standard documents for most Ausländer appointments.

fallback.heading: Details
fallback.body: Select a card to load detailed guidance.

eligibility.heading: Eligibility checklist
eligibility.body: Requirements vary by visa type and city, but these are the most common criteria.
eligibility.item1: Valid passport for entire permit period

documents.heading: Required documents
documents.body: Bring originals plus copies. Missing items can delay your appointment.

process.heading: Application steps
process.step1: Book an appointment at your Ausländerbehörde
process.step4: Receive Fiktionsbescheinigung if processing takes time

appointment.heading: Booking your appointment
appointment.body: Appointments are city-specific. Book early — slots fill quickly.
appointment.tip1: Check your city's online appointment system first
appointment.tip2: Have your passport number ready when booking

fees.heading: Typical fees
fees.body: Fees depend on permit type and duration. Payment is usually at the appointment.
fees.item2: Extensions and special cases may cost more

mistakes.heading: Avoid these common mistakes
mistakes.body: These are the most frequent reasons for delays or second appointments.
mistakes.item1: Missing Anmeldung before your Ausländer appointment

timeline.heading: Typical timeline
timeline.body: Timelines vary by city workload and your case complexity.

anmeldungWhat.heading: City registration (Anmeldung)
anmeldungWhat.body: You must register your address at the Bürgeramt within 14 days of moving in. You'll receive a Meldebescheinigung — needed for bank accounts, permits, and more.

anmeldungDeadline.heading: The 14-day rule
anmeldungDeadline.body: Registration is legally required within two weeks of moving into your new address.
anmeldungDeadline.tip: Late registration can result in fines in some cities.

entry.title: What do you want to navigate?
entry.subtitle: Pick a goal below or ask a specific question to get started.

navigator.cardsHint: Each step is a card. Tap to expand — your full guide stays right here.

step6.title: Ready to build your checklist

bubble.flowSubtitle: Follow the path left to right. Each answer creates the next bubble.
bubble.noOptions: I could not verify current options for this bubble from the AI/RAG service. Please retry, go back, or use the action workspace.

entry.question: Are you in Germany or currently planning to move to Germany?
entry.subtitle: This first choice shapes the rest of your path.
entry.currentHint: Use this if you need help with Anmeldung, a current permit, renewal, or work rights.
entry.planning: I am planning to move to Germany
entry.planningHint: Use this if you are comparing visas before you arrive.

currentDuration.question: How long have you been in Germany?
currentDuration.subtitle: This helps separate first-arrival tasks from renewal or status-change tasks.
currentDuration.justArrivedHint: Good for Anmeldung, first insurance, and first permit steps.
currentDuration.fewMonthsHint: Good for follow-up appointments, missing documents, or first renewals.
currentDuration.settledHint: Good for renewals, status changes, rights, and long-term planning.

currentStatus.question: What is your current visa or residence status?
currentStatus.subtitle: Pick the closest match. You can choose not sure if it is unclear.

currentGoal.question: What do you want to solve next?
currentGoal.subtitle: Your goal decides which document checklist and action cards matter most.

planningAge.question: How old will you be when you apply?
planningAge.subtitle: Age can change which routes are more realistic, especially study, training, and opportunity-card paths.

planningVisa.question: Which visa path should we explore?
planningVisa.subtitle: Options are checked against your answers and filtered before they appear.

visas.studentHint: For university study or preparatory study programs.
visas.vocationalHint: For Ausbildung, apprenticeships, and training contracts.
visas.skilledWorkHint: For recognized qualifications and a qualified job offer.
visas.blueCardHint: For qualified employment with salary and degree requirements.
visas.opportunityHint: For job search when you meet points or qualification criteria.
visas.languageHint: For intensive German courses before another route.
visas.familyHint: For joining a spouse, parent, child, or close family member.
visas.familyMinorHint: For minors moving with or joining parents or guardians in Germany.
visas.schoolHint: For minors, this needs a guardian, school context, and careful official review.
visas.selfEmploymentHint: For founders, freelancers, and business activity.
visas.protectionHint: For protection needs. A counselor can help with this sensitive route.
visas.counselorHint: For a child or unclear case, get human support before choosing a visa path.

readiness.question: What do you already have for that plan?
readiness.subtitle: This tells the AI whether to recommend applying now or preparing first.
readiness.universityAdmissionHint: You already have admission or a conditional admission.
readiness.jobOfferHint: You have an employer or a concrete contract path.
readiness.trainingContractHint: You have an Ausbildung or apprenticeship offer.
readiness.familyInvitationHint: You plan to join a family member in Germany.
readiness.schoolAcceptanceHint: You have a school, exchange, or supervised program lead.
readiness.guardianSupportHint: A parent or legal guardian can support the application.
readiness.exploringHint: You need to compare routes before choosing one.

documents.question: Which documents do you already have?
documents.subtitle: Select all that apply. Empty is okay if you are just starting.
documents.planningQuestion: Which documents do you already have for your planned move?
documents.planningSubtitle: This becomes part of the final AI context.

ai.question: Ready for AI to map your next steps?
ai.subtitle: The AI receives your complete bubble trail as context and answers with official-source guidance when the backend is available.
ai.empty: Your trail is ready. Generate the guide to turn these answers into a tailored plan.
